lede: A look at March 31 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Reed Baker-Whiting and Lyra McKee, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Selena
events.desc: Selena is murdered by her fan club president Yolanda Saldívar at a Days Inn in Corpus Christi, Texas.

events.title: TAROM Flight 371
events.desc: TAROM Flight 371, an Airbus A310-300, crashes near Balotesti, Romania, killing all 60 people on board.

events.title: USS Missouri (BB-63)
events.desc: The USS Missouri, the last active United States Navy battleship, is decommissioned in Long Beach, California.

events.title: Mexicana de Aviación Flight 940
events.desc: Mexicana de Aviación Flight 940 crashes into the Sierra Madre Oriental mountain range near the Mexican town of Maravatío, killing 167.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Japanese forces invade Christmas Island, then a British possession.

events.title: Royal Australian Air Force
events.desc: The Royal Australian Air Force is formed.

events.title: Philippine–American War
events.desc: Philippine–American War: Malolos, capital of the First Philippine Republic, is captured by American forces.

events.title: 1761 Lisbon earthquake
events.desc: The 1761 Lisbon earthquake strikes off the Iberian Peninsula with an estimated magnitude of 8.5, six years after another quake destroyed the city.

events.title: Ferdinand Magellan
events.desc: Ferdinand Magellan and fifty of his men came ashore to present-day Limasawa to participate in the first Catholic mass in the Philippines.

events.title: Ferdinand II of Aragon
events.desc: Ferdinand II of Aragon and Isabella I of Castile sign the Edict of Expulsion of the Jews from Spain, ordering all Jews in their kingdoms to either convert to Christianity or leave the country.

births.name: Mira Bellwether
births.line: American author, artist, and sex educator (died 2022)

deaths.text: Stephen Lewis, Canadian politician and diplomat, 14th Canadian Ambassador to the United Nations
J. P. Morgan, American banker and financier

observances.text: Christian feast day Acathius of Melitene (Eastern Orthodox Church)
Christian feast day John Donne (Anglican Communion, Lutheran)
